And it seems like the people who are responsible for SEO, like they're not even, they're not on the meetings.
And the people who are going to be reviewing the topics as well haven't been on the meetings.
So it's just a lot of people that we're communicating with.
Yeah, the organization is so huge that even the people that they want to review aren't in the meeting.

I think.
What, sorry, why isn't, yeah, she's jumping on in a sec.
Why isn't the person who's reviewing the clusters in that meeting?
No, no, no, the people reviewing the clusters are, I think it was the personas.

Yeah, but also the content calendar, they said that Ralph, who is, like, who has, is doing the work with researching keywords for their internal content strategy, he needs to review our content calendar, and he's not on the calls.
So that's just one thing to know, yeah.
We have Sydney here, maybe afterwards we should have, like, a quick chat about NetRix and their SEO team and things, but we could do that later as well.
Hey, Sydney.
Yeah, one bit of advice that I got from Andy was, because I chatted with her, I think, was it earlier this week or last week, and...
And the NetRix thing came up, like we were in week two at the time, think, or week three, still pretty early with NetRix.

And she was like, listen, my advice is if you see a red flag or even a yellow flag like early on, immediately deal with it, which is why I'm also now starting to really think about, well, what you're telling me is worrying me even more.
But then the Marielle thing is another layer.
And, you know, Tyler's messaging me directly and saying like this, my gut feeling is that this is going to be an issue.
So I think we need to, and obviously you two are more on the ground, so I don't want to speak for you here.

But I do want to say like, broadly speaking, these are things that we need to kind of have a little, you know, battle room to go over and like really discuss like, what are we going to do with NetRix in order to manage this?
Because really, it sounds like it's not at all an ideal situation and this could tip over pretty easily.
Like it's on a house of cards with a lot of different people, a lot of different perspectives, and that's not going to work.
Yeah, yeah.
I think the thing is that even with the review process, they have different people whose names they're using, and there's like a review process that leadership doesn't even know how it works.

So they, you know, once we send this article, and we say, okay, who's going to publish, like there could be multiple people that then start reviewing, like the person whose author's name is being used, that that person is likely going to review.
There's just a lot here.

I mean, the leadership team, like they were on board with the strategy, they want, like, they like the approach, and they just, like what we heard from them today is like, let's just get this thing going, like our traffic's going down, and we want to start publishing.
So that's good.
Like we have, that's a green flag from them that they want us to keep moving.
And I think, yeah, and I think Kathy.
When you send them their post-meeting note today, I think it would be helpful just to tell them, like, hey, next week we want to start working on three articles.
These are the ones that we recommend, like, that we, or actually, no, we just tell them next week we're going to start working on three articles.
And just be like, you know, our goal is to scale, is to get as many shots at goal in these eight weeks that we have together.
And to help you, like, reduce paid search spend, get your traffic, like, recovered, stuff like that.
So that, you know, they understand that we are also on their side.
Yeah.

We want to share them now so that in week seven, this is not now a major issue.
So I think I'll talk with him next week, early in the week to Tyler to like brainstorm this.

But also, like, if you guys want to send me bullet points of all the things that you're concerned about and these elements that you're telling me, Aida, of like certain people that should be on the call, they're not on the call.
The complexity and sort of lack of clarity on who's like reviewing the content, who's responsible for that.
I need all of that information so we can bring that directly to John and say, hey, like, we need this sorted out on your end.

Yes.

So that's another important thing.
Yeah, give me all of that.

I'm going to have another two or three interviews next week, and we're going to be bringing on some more EMs to amplify our capabilities.
Some core things that you all need to be aware of, which maybe you already are, but it's good to say it out loud.
There is significant pressure to improve our conversion rate and at the same time to start to do more kickoffs per week.

And then the conversion rate needs to get up to a minimum of 80%, but ideally 85%.
Last month in December, we were at 73%, so we still have a little bit of a ways to go.
The good news is in January, we had 100% conversion rate, so that's great.
We need to see what it looks like in February.
But that gives you kind of the context of where we are today and what...
Needs to happen.
So broadly speaking, in terms of like structure, I think the first thing y'all need to know is anything that is like majorly wrong, just come to me and then I can always escalate it if it needs to.
Otherwise, we're going to start to build up these systems.
I know this is all brand new, but I think there's a core element here where we need to understand who we can escalate things to.
So I'm just I'm putting this out here right now, like escalate it to me.
And if I can't solve it, then I will find a way to solve it.

My main goal, if in any team I work with is just to be the the best unblocker I can be like, that's my that's like written on top of my desk, be the best unblocker you can be like, literally, that's all I want to do is to make sure that you guys are not getting frustrated or getting blocked, and that you're able to to just do your jobs as smoothly as possible.
But there is going to be an on ramp, as we start to hire.
And that does mean that there's going to be at least for the next month and a half or so, still a balance.

of me managing my clients while also working with you guys.

also want to talk a little bit about lines of communication between the EMs and the MEs, and then if you have questions also beyond that, right?

Do we take it to Kyle?

Cool.

done...

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Restructure the Sprint team and formalize processes to improve performance.](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=381.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**NetRix is a critical risk:** A chaotic client review process is blocking content. William will escalate directly to CMO John to resolve it.](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=275.0)
  - [**Sprint team goals are aggressive:** Increase weekly kickoffs to 4 (from 3) and raise the conversion rate to 80–85% (from 73% in Dec).](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=429.0)
  - [**Process overhaul is the top priority:** The team will replace the unused Linear PM tool and formalize all workflows (handoffs, reporting) to scale operations.](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=1440.0)
  - [**William is the new team lead:** His primary role is to unblock the team, with a transition from his current EM duties over the next 1–2 months.](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=381.0)

## Topics

### NetRix Client Risk

  - [**Problem:** A chaotic review process with multiple stakeholders and missing reviewers is blocking content production.](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=6.0)
  - [**Impact:** This "house of cards" situation could derail the engagement.](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=172.0)
  - [**Solution:** William will escalate directly to John (CMO) to force a resolution.](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=275.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Proactive escalation is required to prevent a major issue in later weeks of the sprint.](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=275.0)
  - [**Immediate Action:** The team will push to start 3 articles next week to maintain momentum and demonstrate value.](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=220.0)

### Sprint Team Restructuring & Goals

  - [**New Lead:** William is the new Sprint team lead, transitioning from EM duties over 1–2 months.](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=381.0)
      - [**Role:** Primary unblocker for the team.](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=508.0)
      - [**Escalation Path:** Team → William → Panzer.](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=579.0)
  - [**Hiring:** New EMs are being hired to increase team capacity.](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=418.0)
  - [**Performance Goals:**](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=429.0)
      - [**Kickoffs:** Increase from 3 to 4 per week.](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=435.0)
      - [**Conversion Rate:** Raise to 80–85% (from 73% in Dec; Jan was 100%).](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=435.0)

### Process Overhaul: Project Management

  - [**Problem:** The current PM tool, Linear, is unused due to its complexity.](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=1464.0)
  - [**Solution:** Replace Linear with a user-friendly alternative.](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=1516.0)
      - [**Rationale:** A tool that is actually used is essential for tracking artifacts (Content OS, etc.) and scaling operations.](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=1913.0)
      - [**Decision:** The team will research alternatives and decide next week.](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=1556.0)
  - [**Alternative Idea:** Build a custom tool using Claude code (Panzer's recent EM tracking tool was cited as an example).](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=1606.0)
      - [**Counterpoint:** A low-cost SaaS tool (\~$300/mo) is likely faster and more efficient than building from scratch.](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=1628.0)

### Process Overhaul: Workflows & Responsibilities

  - [**Goal:** Formalize all workflows to scale operations and reduce Aida's workload.](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=734.0)
  - [**Key Areas for Improvement:**](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=781.0)
      - [**ME Responsibilities:** Proactively manage content status in Airtable, check for client feedback, and update Looker dashboards.](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=1013.0)
      - [**Weekly Updates:** MEs must provide structured updates on content status (published, in review).](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=1013.0)
      - [**Handoffs:** Document all handoffs (client onboarding, sprint-to-full engagement) to prevent breakdowns.](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=1712.0)
      - [**Templates:** Use existing templates (Marisol's scripts) for client communication.](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=1784.0)
  - [**Automation Plan (Sydney):**](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=1087.0)
      - [**Draft Consolidation:** Create one channel for all drafts from Airtable.](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=1087.0)
      - [**Status Reminders:** Send internal reminders on Wednesday/Friday for items in "production" or "client review."](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=1087.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Aida & Kathy:** Send William a bulleted list of NetRix process issues for escalation.](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=313.0)
  - [**William:**](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=275.0)
      - [Strategize with Tyler on the NetRix escalation plan.](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=275.0)
      - [Schedule a call with John (NetRix CMO) to address process blockers.](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=275.0)
      - [Share Marisol's client communication templates with the team.](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=1784.0)
  - [**All:** Research PM tool alternatives to Linear and present options next week.](https://fathom.video/share/iGwpzFPAfDaqx1HNrSJhU8c7xVSvoxLd?tab=summary&timestamp=1556.0)

action_items.description: Send NetRix post-meeting note to client: start 3 articles next week
Collect NetRix issues from Aida/Kathy/Sydney; schedule call w/ John (CMO) + Tyler
Add Aida/Kathy/Sydney to sprint improvement doc; share Marisol scripts; request PM tool recs
Create editing guidelines doc for MEs
